outline.text: Settings → Data Sources & Integrations → + Add New → and enter your VCS data source in the search bar
Settings → Data Sources & Integrations → + Add New
User Settings → Personal access tokens → + New Token
Verify integration: On Data Sources, select Code Providers → GitHub SaaS → View more and confirm that the status of your integrated GitHub instance is 'Connected
Settings → Data Sources & Integrations → hover over a CI tool → View Details
Delete an instance: Right-click on an instance of the CI tool → Delete instance → Delete
If needed, retrieve your public API URL by navigating to Settings → Configurations → API Keys and clicking Copy API URL, then paste it into the code
Windows installer → uninstall via Settings → Apps → Installed apps, or delete the
2.6.1.1. Semgrep Software Composition Analysis (SCA) data ingestion
2.6.1.2. Semgrep Static Application Security Testing (SAST) data ingestion
2.6.2.2. Snyk Static Application Security Testing (SAST) data ingestion
Settings → Data Sources & Integrations → 3rd Party AppSec Collector
Settings → Data Sources → 3rd Party AppSec Collector
2.8.1. Set up a Transporter applet on Broker VM
your AppSec Transporter under the App column → Configure → Add Connection (on the redirected AppSec Transporter setup page
the Appsec Transporter under the App column → Deactivate → Confirm when prompted All existing connections are deleted but their configurations are saved in the database
the Appsec Transporter under the App column → Deactivate → Confirm when prompted
2.8.2. Set up a Transporter on your VCS
Settings → Data Sources & Integrations → + Add New → , and search for your VCS on-premises data source
2.9. Manage integrations via data source APIs
API key pair: Generate an API key (Key ID + Key Secret) from Settings → Configurations → API Keys in the Cortex Cloud tenant
You can view AppSec Admin permissions in the tenant by navigating to Settings → Configurations → Roles → AppSec Admin

Full Code to Cloud (C2C) coverage is achieved when Cortex Cloud can resolve the following chain: Repository → Pipeline → Image → (optional Registry) → Runtime resources (including VMs, VM images, and IaC-defined infrastructure
4.1.2. Code to Cloud context and visibility
Topology graph: Located in the Business Applications side card, this tab visualizes the entire path to production (Code → Build → Deploy → Run), allowing you
4.3.1.2. Manage Criteria via the tenant UI
Modules → Application Security → Application Management → Criteria
4.3.1.4. Define applications by cloud tag-based Criteria
4.3.1.5. Manage criteria via the public API
4.3.1.6. How to manually build an application
Inventory → All Assets → Business Applications (under Application
4.3.5. Scope user access to applications (Application SBAC)
Configure user scopes in Cortex Cloud by navigating to Settings → Configurations → Access Management
4.3.5.1. Enable SBAC in the Cortex Cloud tenant
Settings → Configurations → General → Server Settings → Enable Scope Based Access Control
4.3.5.3. Scope user access to an application
Settings → Configurations → User Groups (under Access Management
Right-click on a group in the table → Edit Group → select the Scope tab
Assets → Select asset groups → select an Asset Group associated with applications
the chevron icon (>) in the Assets field → Select Asset groups → select the user group scoped to the application (see above
Modules → Application Security → AppSec Polices (under Policy Management) → Add Policy
Add Filter → Business Application Names → enter the required application name
4.4.1. Understanding repository assets via the UI
To access repository assets, under Inventory, select All Assets → Code → Repositories

From the Repositories table, right-click a repository → Open in Agentic Assistant → select Application Security from the agents menu, and query repository-specific insights (for example, scan coverage, risk posture, or gaps
4.6.1. Urgency and code to cloud traceability

Settings → Configuration → Application Security → AppSec Issues Configurations and enable Are SBOM issues considered new Permissions for all other capabilities, such as viewing issues or applying policies, are defined by the existing RBAC/SBAC policies and the user's specific issue management capabilities
Settings → Configuration → Application Security → AppSec Issues Configurations and enable Are SBOM issues considered new
4.8.1. Configure and monitor Cortex Cloud Application Security SLAs
Settings → Configurations → Application Configuration (under Application Security
4.9. Compliance for Cortex Cloud Application Security
Modules → Application Security → AppSec Polices → Add Policy
Modules → Application Security → IaC Misconfigurations (under Issues
Posture Management → Rules & Policies → Policy → Application Security The policy table displays all policies (out-of-the-box (OOTB) and custom
Posture Management → Rules & Policies → Policy → Application Security
4.10.10. Reference B: Condition filters and logic
4.10.12. Reference D: Trigger and actions mapping
4.10.13. Reference E: Grace period logic and configuration
4.10.14. Reference F: Engine evaluation and Urgency logic
4.10.15. Reference G: Finding type to trigger mapping
4.10.16. Reference H: Action availability by trigger
Settings → Configurations → Management → Audit Logs to review policy audit entries
To filter rules relating to Secrets, select filter icon → Scanner (from the Select field) → Secrets (from the Value field
4.11.3. Create custom Cortex Cloud Application Security rules
4.12.1. Navigate to SAST code weakness issues
To access SAST code violation issues, under Modules, select Application Security → Issues → Code Weaknesses
4.12.3. Investigate and remediate code weakness issues
From the main issues table: Right-click on an issue in the table → Change Status → [Select a status
From the main issues table: Right-click on an issue in the table → Change Assignee → [Select a user
From the main issues table: Right-click on an issue in the table → Change Severity → [Select a severity
To access VCS organization assets, under Inventory, select All Assets → Code → VCS Organizations
5.1.3.1. Understanding repository assets via the UI
5.1.5.2. Investigate software package assets and security issues
To access CI/CD instance assets, under Inventory, select All Assets → Code → CI/CD Instances
5.1.6.2. Investigate and manage CI/CD instance assets
the dedicated Application Security → Issues → CI/CD Risks page
To access CI/CD pipeline assets, under Inventory, select All Assets → Code → CI/CD Pipelines
5.1.7.2. Investigate and manage CI/CD pipeline assets
From the CI/CD Pipelines table, right-click a pipeline row → Open in Agentic Assistant → Application Security from the agents menu
In the inventory table, right-click on a tool → Change Status → select a status
To access Supply Chain Tools, select Modules → Application Security → Supply Chain Tools (under 3rd Party Tools
To access the Supply Chain Catalog, select Modules → Application Security → Supply Chain Catalog (under 3rd party tools
5.2.1. Supported Software Composition Analysis (SCA) frameworks and languages
5.2.2.2. Investigate and remediate CVE vulnerability issues
To access CVE findings, under Modules, select Application Security → Issues → Vulnerabilities → click the Findings tab
5.2.3.2. Take action on license miscompliance issues
To access license miscompliance findings, under Modules, select Application Security → Issues → Licenses → click the Findings tab
To access CI/CD pipeline risks issues, under Modules, select Application Security → Issues → CI/CD Risks
5.3.3. VCS and CI/CD pipeline risk findings
5.4.1. CI/CD policies user roles and permissions
5.4.3.1. Cortex Cloud Application Security CI/CD policy Condition attributes
You can view the granular permissions assigned to each role in the tenant by navigating to Settings → Configurations → Roles
6.2.1. Supported Software Composition Analysis (SCA) frameworks and languages
To access software package assets, under Inventory, select All Assets → Code → Software Packages
6.2.3. Investigate software package assets and security issues
Inventory, select All Assets → Code → IaC Resources
Under Modules, select Application Security → Issues → Secrets
To access IaC misconfiguration issues, under Modules, select Application Security → Issues → IaC misconfiguration
6.6.3. Investigate and remediate IaC misconfiguration issues
To access IaC misconfiguration findings, under Modules, select Application Security → Issues → IaC Misconfigurations → click the Findings tab
The Cortex Cloud Application Security IaC misconfigurations Findings table is a filtered view of the comprehensive Findings inventory, located at Cases & Issues → Issues → Findings tab
6.7.1. Navigate to IaC drift detection issues
6.7.2. Understand the IaC drift detection table
6.7.3. Investigate and remediate IaC drift issues
From the main drift table:Right-click on a drift issue in the table → Change Status → Change Status
From the main drift table:Right-click on a drift issue in the table → Change Assignee → Change Status
Modules → Application Security → IaC Drifts (under Issues) → click the Findings tab
6.8.1.3. Investigate and remediate CVE vulnerability issues
6.8.2.3. Take action on license miscompliance issues
6.8.2.4. How license miscompliance issues fit in the Application Security ecosystem
Configure unified policies for license compliance at Modules → Application Security → AppSec Policies
6.8.3.1. Understand the package operational risk table
6.8.3.2. Investigate and remediate package operational risk issues
6.8.3.3. How package operational risk issues fit in the Application Security ecosystem
Configure unified policies for operational risk at Modules → Application Security → AppSec Policies
6.9. API workflows for Code Security issues
Generate an API key pair from Settings → Configurations → API Keys in the Cortex Cloud console
6.12.2. Visual Studio (VS) Code and VS Code compatible IDEs
Settings → Configurations → API Keys (under Integration) → click Copy API URL
Install through VS Code IDE Access the Activity bar → Extensions → Cortex Cloud → Install
Access the Activity bar → Extensions → Cortex Cloud → Install
6.12.2.1. How to use the Cortex Cloud extension in VS Code
Settings → Plugins → select the Marketplace tab → search for Prisma Cloud → Install → OK
6.12.3.1. How to use the JetBrains Cortex Cloud extension
To authenticate, you must generate a dedicated API key in the Cortex Cloud console under Settings → Configurations → API Keys
